text:
*[Bug 18928] Missing pieces in the C API for environments*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18928>

--- Comment #7 from Lionel Henry (<mailto:lionel.hry@gmail.com|lionel.hry@gmail.com>) ---
Hi Luke, thanks a lot for working on this!

In <https://github.com/r-lib/rlang/pull/1876> I've noticed a bug in the promise
accessors (delayed and forced) and in the type getter. All of these should
follow promise chains (when the PREXPR is itself a PROMSXP). Otherwise the
accessors will return promise objects, which we'd like to avoid as these should
not be exposed to package code.

See how I've implemented the polyfills in rlang:

- type getter:
<https://github.com/r-lib/rlang/blob/aa178475b08dd4510d8ebe26c4f5d4441909ad37/src/rlang/env-binding.c#L116-L153>
- promise getters:
<https://github.com/r-lib/rlang/blob/aa178475b08dd4510d8ebe26c4f5d4441909ad37/src/rlang/env-binding.c#L225-L288>

In particular note the use of `rlang_promise_unwrap()` in these
implementations. The promise unwrapper is implemented here:
<https://github.com/r-lib/rlang/blob/aa178475b08dd4510d8ebe26c4f5d4441909ad37/src/rlang/rlang-types.h#L141-L159>


I was going to submit an updated patch. I can do so based on the recent
additions if you wish, just let me know if you'd like that.

The same approach will be needed for dots (see same rlang PR, I'll send a patch
once merged).

Once r-devel has updated accessors, I'll gate the polyfill for R < 4.6 and run
the rlang tests (which are very comprehensive) on r-devel.

text:
*[Bug 18928] Missing pieces in the C API for environments*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18928>

--- Comment #8 from Lionel Henry (<mailto:lionel.hry@gmail.com|lionel.hry@gmail.com>) ---
Here are more test cases for binding-type:

```
    # Promise chains (nested forwarding)
    f6 <- function(x) getBindingType("x", environment())
    f6_outer <- function(x) f6(x)
    stopifnot(f6_outer(1) == "delayed")

    # Promise chain where inner promise is forced
    f7 <- function(x) getBindingType("x", environment())
    f7_outer <- function(x) { force(x); f7(x) }
    stopifnot(f7_outer(1) == "forced")

    # Deeper promise chain
    f8 <- function(x) getBindingType("x", environment())
    f8_mid <- function(x) f8(x)
    f8_outer <- function(x) f8_mid(x)
    stopifnot(f8_outer(1) == "delayed")

    # Deeper chain with force at the top
    f9 <- function(x) getBindingType("x", environment())
    f9_mid <- function(x) f9(x)
    f9_outer <- function(x) { force(x); f9_mid(x) }
    stopifnot(f9_outer(1) == "forced")
```

Note that f6 and f8 do pass, but by chance, because the inner promise is of the
right type. For promise accessors, you'd get wrong results without the promise
unwrapping.

elements.text: [Bug 18928] Missing pieces in the C API for environments

) ---
Here are more test cases for binding-type:

# Promise chains (nested forwarding)
    f6 <- function(x) getBindingType("x", environment())
    f6_outer <- function(x) f6(x)
    stopifnot(f6_outer(1) == "delayed")

    # Promise chain where inner promise is forced
    f7 <- function(x) getBindingType("x", environment())
    f7_outer <- function(x) { force(x); f7(x) }
    stopifnot(f7_outer(1) == "forced")

    # Deeper promise chain
    f8 <- function(x) getBindingType("x", environment())
    f8_mid <- function(x) f8(x)
    f8_outer <- function(x) f8_mid(x)
    stopifnot(f8_outer(1) == "delayed")

    # Deeper chain with force at the top
    f9 <- function(x) getBindingType("x", environment())
    f9_mid <- function(x) f9(x)
    f9_outer <- function(x) { force(x); f9_mid(x) }
    stopifnot(f9_outer(1) == "forced")

Note that f6 and f8 do pass, but by chance, because the inner promise is of the
right type. For promise accessors, you'd get wrong results without the promise
unwrapping.

text:
*[Bug 18928] Missing pieces in the C API for environments*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18928>

--- Comment #11 from Luke Tierney (<mailto:luke@stat.uiowa.edu|luke@stat.uiowa.edu>) ---
None of these actually produce wrapped promises. The main way those happen is
via expanding `...` into a call. So with

```
f <- function(...) g(...)
g <- function(x) x
```

the value in the binding for `x` in the call to `g()` in a call `f(1)` will be
a wrapped promise. There may be a few other ways this can happen, aside from C
code doing it on purpose. I've thought about trying to prevent it, for example
by having `mkPROMISE` just return `expr` if it is a promise, but I haven't
pursued it.

One small worry about unwrapping is that someone could create a promise loop.
Might be worth guarding against using Floyd's algorithm.

Thanks for the patch; I'll hopefully to commit later today.

text:
*[Bug 18928] Missing pieces in the C API for environments*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18928>

--- Comment #12 from Lionel Henry (<mailto:lionel.hry@gmail.com|lionel.hry@gmail.com>) ---
Of course, sorry about that. Wrapped up too fast at the end of the day.

Here are updated tests:

```
## R_GetBindingType with promise chains from `...` expansion (PR#18928)
## Expanding `...` into named parameters creates promise chains where
## PRCODE of the wrapper is the original PROMSXP
local({
    getBindingType <- function(sym, env) {
        if (is.character(sym))
            sym <- as.name(sym)
        .Internal(getBindingType(sym, env))
    }
    g <- function(x) getBindingType("x", environment())
    # `...` expansion into a named parameter wraps the dot promise
    x <- 1
    f <- function(...) g(...)
    stopifnot(f(x) == "delayed")
    # Forcing the dot then expanding: unwrapping must detect the forced inner
    f <- function(...) { force(..1); g(...) }
    stopifnot(f(x) == "forced")
    # Deeper chain: `...` -> `...` -> named parameter
    mid <- function(...) g(...)
    f <- function(...) mid(...)
    stopifnot(f(x) == "delayed")
    # Force at the top of a deeper chain
    f <- function(...) { force(..1); mid(...) }
    stopifnot(f(x) == "forced")
})

```

Now what I don't understand: on R devel, all of these tests actually pass with
and without the promise unwrapping step. But for the polyfills in rlang, I
definitely need promise unwrapping to get equivalent tests to pass, including
on R devel.

Do you know what that might be the case?

elements.text: [Bug 18928] Missing pieces in the C API for environments

## R_GetBindingType with promise chains from `...` expansion (PR#18928)
## Expanding `...` into named parameters creates promise chains where
## PRCODE of the wrapper is the original PROMSXP
local({
    getBindingType <- function(sym, env) {
        if (is.character(sym))
            sym <- as.name(sym)
        .Internal(getBindingType(sym, env))
    }
    g <- function(x) getBindingType("x", environment())
    # `...` expansion into a named parameter wraps the dot promise
    x <- 1
    f <- function(...) g(...)
    stopifnot(f(x) == "delayed")
    # Forcing the dot then expanding: unwrapping must detect the forced inner
    f <- function(...) { force(..1); g(...) }
    stopifnot(f(x) == "forced")
    # Deeper chain: `...` -> `...` -> named parameter
    mid <- function(...) g(...)
    f <- function(...) mid(...)
    stopifnot(f(x) == "delayed")
    # Force at the top of a deeper chain
    f <- function(...) { force(..1); mid(...) }
    stopifnot(f(x) == "forced")
})

Now what I don't understand: on R devel, all of these tests actually pass with
and without the promise unwrapping step. But for the polyfills in rlang, I
definitely need promise unwrapping to get equivalent tests to pass, including
on R devel.

Do you know what that might be the case?

*[Bug 18928] Missing pieces in the C API for environments*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18928>

--- Comment #14 from Lionel Henry (<mailto:lionel.hry@gmail.com|lionel.hry@gmail.com>) ---
> Now what I don't understand: on R devel, all of these tests actually pass with and without the promise unwrapping step. 

Mystery solved: libR wasn't getting updated when I rebuilt without the fix, for
some reason.

I've confirmed that the "forced" tests in the updated patch fail without
promise unwrapping. The "delayed" ones pass in both cases because the type of
inner and outer match in that case.

some reason.

I've confirmed that the "forced" tests in the updated patch fail without
promise unwrapping. The "delayed" ones pass in both cases because the type of
inner and outer match in that case.

--- Comment #16 from Lionel Henry (<mailto:lionel.hry@gmail.com|lionel.hry@gmail.com>) ---
Created attachment 3626
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3626&action=edit>
Unwrap promise chains and detect cycles

Update to the promise unwrapping patch. I added more tests and noticed a bug in
my earlier patch. It was returning the first forced promise it encountered,
instead of continuing following the chain until the last forced promise. That
would cause `R_PromiseExpr()` to return a promise.

Now fixed and includes all the new tests.

Also now uses camelCase `promiseUnwrap()` instead of `promise_unwrap()`
(habits).

text:
*[Bug 19019] New: interaction() should warn when coalescing levels*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19019>

            Bug ID: 19019
           Summary: interaction() should warn when coalescing levels
           Product: R
           Version: R 4.5.x
          Hardware: All
                OS: All
            Status: UNCONFIRMED
          Severity: trivial
          Priority: P5
         Component: Analyses
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:robert.schlicht@tu-dresden.de|robert.schlicht@tu-dresden.de>

In the code for `interaction()` in src/library/base/R/interaction.R, I suggest
adding a warning in the `while(j <- anyDuplicated(ly)) {...}` clause so there
is an indication of what goes wrong in expressions like
```
ave(3:4, c(1.0, 1.5), c(5.5, 5.0))
```
which should return `3 4`, but returns `3.5 3.5`. The issue is the same as the
one mentioned on the help page for `split` at the end of the Details section.
